dialogue_text: Truly I should be ungrateful if I failed to mourn for the man who gave me my lovely heart. I should like to cry a little because Oz is gone, if you will kindly wipe away my tears, so that I shall not rust.
source_excerpt: "Truly I should be ungrateful if I failed to mourn for the man who gave me my lovely heart. I should like to cry a little because Oz is gone, if you will kindly wipe away my tears, so that I shall not rust."

source_excerpt: "I also," said the Tin Woodman, "am well-pleased with my new heart; and, really, that was the only thing I wished in all the world."

dialogue_text: For my part, I am content in knowing I am as brave as any beast that ever lived, if not braver,
source_excerpt: "For my part, I am content in knowing I am as brave as any beast that ever lived, if not braver," said the Lion modestly.

dialogue_text: If Dorothy would only be contented to live in the Emerald City,
source_excerpt: "If Dorothy would only be contented to live in the Emerald City," continued the Scarecrow, "we might all be happy together."

dialogue_text: But I don't want to live here,
source_excerpt: "But I don't want to live here," cried Dorothy. "I want to go to Kansas, and live with Aunt Em and Uncle Henry."
